metadata.summary: Python SDK for the LifeOmic platform

documentation:
# PHC SDK for Python

The phc-sdk-py is a developer kit for interfacing with the
[PHC API](https://api.docs.lifeomic.com/) on Python 3.7 and above.

## Project Status

![GitHub](https://img.shields.io/github/license/lifeomic/phc-sdk-py.svg?style=for-the-badge)
![PyPI status](https://img.shields.io/pypi/status/phc.svg?style=for-the-badge)
![Downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dw/phc?style=for-the-badge)
![GitHub release](https://img.shields.io/github/release/lifeomic/phc-sdk-py.svg?style=for-the-badge)
[![Docs](https://img.shields.io/badge/DOCS-PASSING-green?style=for-the-badge)](https://lifeomic.github.io/phc-sdk-py/)
[![User Guides](https://img.shields.io/badge/DOCS-PASSING-green?style=for-the-badge)](https://devcenter.docs.lifeomic.com/guides/python-sdk)

## Getting Started

### Dependencies

- [Python 3](https://www.python.org/download/releases/3.0/) version >= 3.9

### Getting the Source

This project is [hosted on GitHub](https://github.com/lifeomic/phc-sdk-py).

### Usage

A `Session` needs to be created first that stores the token and account
information needed to access the PHC API. One can currently using API Key tokens
generated from the PHC Account, or OAuth tokens generated using the
[CLI](https://github.com/lifeomic/cli).

```python
from phc import Session

session = Session(token=<TOKEN VALUE>, account="myaccount")
```

Once a `Session` is created, you can then access the different parts of the
platform.

```python
from phc.services import Accounts

accounts = Accounts(session)
myaccounts = accounts.get_list()
```

## Contributing

We encourage public contributions! Please review
[CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) and [CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
for details on our code of conduct and development process.

## License

This project is licensed under the MIT License - see [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for
details.

## Authors

See the list of
[contributors](https://github.com/lifeomic/phc-sdk-py/contributors) who
participate in this project.

## Acknowledgements

This project is built with the following:

- [aiohttp](https://aiohttp.readthedocs.io/en/stable/) - Asynchronous HTTP
  Client/Server for asyncio and Python.

methods.name: phc.services.fhir.Fhir.execute_sql
documentation:
Executes an SQL query against fhir-search-service

# Parameters

project_id

: The project ID.

statement

: The SQL statement.

# Returns

phc.ApiResponse

: The query response.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import pandas as pd
>>> from phc.services import Fhir
>>> fhir = Fhir(session)
>>> res = fhir.execute_sql(project_id='19e34782-91c4-4143-aaee-2ba81ed0b206',
               statement='SELECT * from patient LIMIT 0,5000')
```

```pycon
>>> resources = list(map(lambda r: r.get("_source"), res.get("hits").get("hits")))
>>> df = pd.DataFrame(resources)
```

methods.name: phc.services.analytics.Analytics.execute_sql
documentation:
Executes a SQL query against Analytics

# Parameters

project_id

: The project ID

cohort_id

: The cohort ID

statement

: The SQL statement

# Returns

ApiResponse

: The API Response

# Raises

ValueError

: If no project or cohort ID is provided

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from phc.services import Analytics
>>> client = Analytics(session)
>>> res = client.execute_sql(cohort_id='5a07dedb-fa2a-4cb0-b662-95b23a050221', statement='SELECT patients from patient')
>>> print(f"Found {len(res.get('data').get('patients'))} patients")
```

methods.name: phc.services.analytics.Analytics.get_patients
documentation:
Executes a query that returns patients

# Parameters

project_id

: The project ID

query_builder

: The query builder

# Returns

list

: The list of patients

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from phc.services import Analytics
>>> from phc.util import PatientFilterQueryBuilder
>>> client = Analytics(session)
>>> search = PatientFilterQueryBuilder()
>>> search.patient()                 .observations()                 .code(eq='11142-7')                 .system(eq='http://loinc.org')                 .value_quantity(lt=40)
>>> res = client.get_patients(project='5a07dedb-fa2a-4cb0-b662-95b23a050221', query_builder=search)
>>> print(f"Found {len(res)} patients")
```

Provides acccess to PHC Analytics

Parameters
----------
session : phc.Session
    The PHC session
run_async: bool
    True to return promises, False to return results (default is False)
timeout: int
    Operation timeout (default is 30)
trust_env: bool
    Get proxies information from HTTP_PROXY / HTTPS_PROXY environment variables if the parameter is True (False by default)

methods.name: phc.services.tools.Tools.create
documentation:
Create a tool.

# Parameters

name: str

: The name to give to the tool

description: str

: A description of the tool

access: ToolAccess

: The access level given to the tool [PRIVATE, ACCOUNT, PHC, PUBLIC]

version: str

: The initial version of the tool

tool_class: ToolClass

: The class of the tool [Workflow, Notebook]

source: str

: The path of the tool to upload

labels: List[str], optional

: A list of labels to apply to the tool, i.e. ["bam","samtools"]

# Returns

ApiResponse

: The create tool response

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from phc.services import Tools
>>> tools = Tools(session)
>>> tools.create(name="Read Depth Notebook", description="Generates a chart of positional read depth from a bam file",
      access="PHC", version="1.0.0", tool_class="Notebook", source="./mynotebook.ipynb", labels=["bam","samtools]")
```

methods.name: phc.easy.query.Query.execute_fhir_dsl
documentation:
Execute a FHIR query with the DSL

See <https://devcenter.docs.lifeomic.com/development/fhir-service/dsl>

# Attributes

query

: The FHIR query to run (is a superset of elasticsearch)

all_results

: Return all results by scrolling through mutliple pages of data
  (Limit is ignored if provided)

auth_args

: Additional arguments for authentication

callback

: A progress function that is invoked for each batch. When the second
  argument passed is true, then the result of the callback function is
  used as the return value. This is useful if writing results out to a
  file and then returning the completed result from that file.

  Example:

  > def handle_batch(batch, is_finished):
  >
  > : print(len(batch))
  >   if is_finished:
  >
  >   > return "batch finished

max_pages

: The number of pages to retrieve (useful if working with tons of records)

log

: Whether to log the elasticsearch query sent to the server

query_kwargs

: Arguments to pass to build_queries such as patient_id, patient_ids,
  and patient_key. (See phc.easy.query.fhir_dsl_query.build_queries)

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import phc.easy as phc
>>> phc.Auth.set({ 'account': '<your-account-name>' })
>>> phc.Project.set_current('My Project Name')
>>> phc.Query.execute_fhir_dsl({
  "type": "select",
  "columns": "*",
  "from": [
      {"table": "patient"}
  ],
}, all_results=True)
```

methods.name: phc.easy.query.Query.execute_paging_api
documentation:
Execute a API query that pages through results

# Attributes

path

: The API path to hit
  (Special tokens: `{project_id}`)

params

: The parameters to include with request

http_verb

: The HTTP method to use

all_results

: Retrieve sample of results (25) or entire set of records

auth_args

: Additional arguments for authentication

max_pages

: The number of pages to retrieve (useful if working with tons of records)

page_size

: The number of records to fetch per page

log

: Whether to log some diagnostic statements for debugging

progress

: Override the given progress indicator

item_key

: The key to find the results underneath (usually "items" but not always)

try_count

: Whether to try and send a "count" param to update the progress bar

response_to_items

: Custom function to transform response data to list of items
  (Overrides item_key when present)

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import phc.easy as phc
>>> phc.Auth.set({ 'account': '<your-account-name>' })
>>> phc.Project.set_current('My Project Name')
>>> phc.Query.execute_paging_api(
        "genomics/projects/{project_id}/tests",
        params={
            "patientId": "<patient-uuid>"
        }
    )
```

methods.name: phc.easy.query.Query.get_codes
documentation:
Find FHIR codes with a display for a given table

# Attributes

table_name

: The FHIR Search Service table to retrieve from

code_fields

: The fields of this table that contain a system, code, and display

display_query

: Part of the code's display to match (will try to extract full code
  if passed)

sample_size

: Override the search size for finding codes (may miss codes on later
  records)

kwargs

: Arguments to pass to `phc.easy.query.Query.execute_composite_aggregations`

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import phc.easy as phc
>>> phc.Auth.set({ 'account': '<your-account-name>' })
>>> phc.Project.set_current('My Project Name')
>>> phc.Query.get_codes(
    table_name="observation",
    code_fields=["meta.tag", "code.coding"],
    patient_id="<my-patient-id>"
)
```

methods.name: phc.easy.query.Query.execute_composite_aggregations
documentation:
Count records by multiple fields

# Attributes

table_name

: The FHIR Search Service table to retrieve from

key_sources_pairs

: Pairs of keys and sources to pull composite results from

  Example Input:

  : \[

    : ("meta.tag", [{"terms": {"field": "meta.tag.system.keyword"}}])

    \]

batch_size

: The size of each page from elasticsearch to use

query_overrides

: Parts of the FSS query to override
  (Note that passing certain values can cause the method to error out)

  Example aggregation query executed (can use log=True to inspect):

  : {

    : "type": "select",
      "columns": \[{

      > "type": "elasticsearch",
      > "aggregations": {
      >
      > > "results": {
      > >
      > > : "composite": {
      > >
      > >   : "sources": \[{
      > >
      > >     : "meta.tag": {
      > >
      > >       : "terms": {
      > >
      > >         : "field": "meta.tag.system.keyword"
      > >
      > >         }
      > >
      > >       }
      > >
      > >     }\],
      > >     "size": 100,
      > >
      > >   }
      > >
      > > }
      >
      > },

      }\],
      "from": [{"table": "observation"}],

    }

auth_args

: Additional arguments for authentication

log

: Whether to log the elasticsearch query sent to the server

max_pages

: The number of pages to retrieve (useful if working with tons of records)

query_kwargs

: Arguments to pass to build_queries such as patient_id, patient_ids,
  and patient_key. See {func}`~phc.easy.query.fhir_dsl_query.build_queries`.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import phc.easy as phc
>>> phc.Auth.set({ 'account': '<your-account-name>' })
>>> phc.Project.set_current('My Project Name')
>>> phc.Query.execute_composite_aggregations(
    table_name="observation",
    key_sources_pairs=[
        ("meta.tag", [
            {"code": {"terms": {"field": "meta.tag.code.keyword"}}},
        ]),
        ("code.coding", [
            {"display": {"terms": {"field": "code.coding.display.keyword"}}}
        ]),
    ]
)
```

methods.name: phc.easy.query.Query.get_count_by_field

      > >

      >

      "from": [{"table": "patient"}],

    }

log

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import phc.easy as phc
>>> phc.Auth.set({ 'account': '<your-account-name>' })
```
